quizQuestion:
Cette ville est {___} grande que la mienne.
(This city is bigger than mine.)
sentenceTranslation: This city is bigger than mine.
grammarPointLabel: Comparatif de supériorité: plus + adj + que
explanation: plus…que = comparative of superiority. Structure: plus + adjective + que + second term.

quizQuestion:
Il parle plus vite {___} son frère.
(He speaks faster than his brother.)
sentenceTranslation: He speaks faster than his brother.
grammarPointLabel: Comparatif de supériorité: plus + adj + que
explanation: que links the two terms in a comparison: plus vite que = faster than.

quizQuestion:
Ce livre est {___} intéressant que le film.
(This book is less interesting than the film.)
sentenceTranslation: This book is less interesting than the film.
explanation: moins…que = comparative of inferiority. Structure: moins + adjective + que + second term.

quizQuestion:
Le train est {___} l'avion.
(The train is cheaper than the plane.)
sentenceTranslation: The train is cheaper than the plane.
explanation: moins cher que = cheaper than. Structure: moins + adjective + que + noun.

quizQuestion:
Il t'aime {___} que moi.
(He loves you as much as I do.)
sentenceTranslation: He loves you as much as I do.
explanation: aussi…que = comparative of equality. Structure: aussi + adjective + que + second term.

quizQuestion:
Viens {___} possible.
(Come as soon as possible.)
sentenceTranslation: Come as soon as possible.
explanation: aussi vite que = as fast as. Works with adjectives and adverbs: aussi vite que possible.

quizQuestion:
Ma sœur est {___} mon frère.
(My sister is as intelligent as my brother.)
sentenceTranslation: My sister is as intelligent as my brother.
explanation: aussi intelligente que = as intelligent as. The adjective agrees with the subject: sœur (f.) → intelligente.

quizQuestion:
Paris est {___} Lyon.
(Paris is bigger than Lyon.)
sentenceTranslation: Paris is bigger than Lyon.
grammarPointLabel: Comparatif de supériorité: plus + adj + que
explanation: plus grand que = bigger than. No separate comparative adjective in French — use plus + base adjective.

quizQuestion:
Il a {___} patience que moi.
(He has more patience than me.)
sentenceTranslation: He has more patience than me.
grammarPointLabel: Comparatif avec noms: plus de + nom + que
explanation: For comparing quantities of nouns, use plus de…que (not plus…que). Plus de replaces 'more' before a noun.

quizQuestion:
Elle gagne {___} argent que son patron.
(She earns less money than her boss.)
sentenceTranslation: She earns less money than her boss.
grammarPointLabel: Comparatif avec noms: moins de + nom + que
explanation: moins de…que = less/fewer… than (with nouns). Pattern: moins de + noun + que + second term.

quizQuestion:
Tu as {___} livres que moi.
(You have as many books as I do.)
sentenceTranslation: You have as many books as I do.
grammarPointLabel: Comparatif avec noms: autant de + nom + que
explanation: autant de…que = as much/many… as (with nouns). Note: with nouns use autant de, not aussi.

quizQuestion:
C'est {___} robe du magasin.
(It's the most beautiful dress in the shop.)
sentenceTranslation: It's the most beautiful dress in the shop.
explanation: la plus belle = the most beautiful (f. sg.). Superlative: article (agreeing) + plus/moins + adjective.

quizQuestion:
C'est {___} bâtiment de la ville.
(It's the tallest building in the city.)
sentenceTranslation: It's the tallest building in the city.
explanation: le plus grand = the tallest/biggest (m. sg.). The article agrees in gender and number with the noun.

quizQuestion:
Cherchez {___} hôtel de la région.
(Look for the cheapest hotel in the area.)
sentenceTranslation: Look for the cheapest hotel in the area.
explanation: le moins cher = the cheapest (superlative of inferiority). Use article + moins + adjective.

quizQuestion:
Ce sont {___} coureurs du monde.
(They are the fastest runners in the world.)
sentenceTranslation: They are the fastest runners in the world.
explanation: les plus rapides = the fastest (pl.). Plural superlative: les (pl.) + plus + adjective in plural form.

quizQuestion:
C'est le restaurant le plus cher {___} la ville.
(It's the most expensive restaurant in the city.)
sentenceTranslation: It's the most expensive restaurant in the city.
grammarPointLabel: Superlatif + groupe: le plus + adj + de + groupe
explanation: After the superlative, the group/scope is introduced by de: le plus cher de la ville = the most expensive in the city.

quizQuestion:
Ce café est {___} que celui d'hier.
(This coffee is better than yesterday's.)
sentenceTranslation: This coffee is better than yesterday's.
explanation: meilleur = better (comparative of bon/good). Never say plus bon — only meilleur is correct.

quizQuestion:
Sa pizza est {___} que la mienne.
(Her pizza is better than mine.)
sentenceTranslation: Her pizza is better than mine.
explanation: meilleure = better (f. sg.). meilleur agrees in gender: bon (m.) → meilleur / bonne (f.) → meilleure.

quizQuestion:
Jean est mon {___} ami.
(Jean is my best friend.)
sentenceTranslation: Jean is my best friend.
explanation: meilleur ami = best friend. When used with a possessive (mon, ma), no article needed: mon meilleur ami.

quizQuestion:
Espérons {___}.
(Let's hope for the best.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's hope for the best.
explanation: le meilleur = the best (m. sg. superlative of bon). Superlative form uses definite article: le/la/les meilleur(e)(s).

quizQuestion:
Cette situation est {___} qu'avant.
(This situation is worse than before.)
sentenceTranslation: This situation is worse than before.
explanation: pire = worse (comparative of mauvais/bad). pire is preferred; plus mauvais is also acceptable but less common.

quizQuestion:
C'est {___} film que j'aie jamais vu.
(It's the worst film I've ever seen.)
sentenceTranslation: It's the worst film I've ever seen.
explanation: le pire = the worst (m. sg. superlative of mauvais). Superlative: le/la/les pire(s).

quizQuestion:
{___} vaut tard que jamais.
(Better late than never.)
sentenceTranslation: Better late than never.
explanation: mieux = better (comparative of bien/well, adverb). Never say plus bien — only mieux is correct.

quizQuestion:
Je chante {___} que toi.
(I sing better than you.)
sentenceTranslation: I sing better than you.
explanation: mieux = better (adverb). It compares how well someone does something. mieux que = better than.

quizQuestion:
Il parle {___} que sa sœur.
(He speaks less well than his sister.)
sentenceTranslation: He speaks less well than his sister.
grammarPointLabel: Comparatif d'infériorité de bien: moins bien
explanation: moins bien = less well. For the adverb bien, inferiority uses moins bien (not pire, which is for mauvais).

quizQuestion:
Ce vin est {___} que celui-là. (adj — comparer le vin)
(This wine is better than that one.)
sentenceTranslation: This wine is better than that one.
explanation: meilleur = better as an adjective (replaces bon). It modifies a noun and agrees: meilleur / meilleure / meilleurs / meilleures.

quizQuestion:
Elle joue au tennis {___} que moi. (adv — comparer comment elle joue)
(She plays tennis better than me.)
sentenceTranslation: She plays tennis better than me.
explanation: mieux = better as an adverb (replaces bien). It modifies a verb and never changes form: joue mieux = plays better.

quizQuestion:
C'est {___} question de l'examen.
(It's the least difficult question on the exam.)
sentenceTranslation: It's the least difficult question on the exam.
grammarPointLabel: Superlatif d'infériorité: la + moins + adj (f. sg.)
explanation: la moins difficile = the least difficult (f. sg.). Article must agree with the noun: la question → la moins difficile.

quizQuestion:
La voiture est {___} le vélo.
(The car is faster than the bicycle.)
sentenceTranslation: The car is faster than the bicycle.
grammarPointLabel: Comparatif de supériorité: plus + adj + que
explanation: plus rapide que = faster than. French uses plus + base adjective (rapide) + que — no separate comparative form.

quizQuestion:
Ce sont {___} chaussures du magasin.
(They are the least expensive shoes in the shop.)
sentenceTranslation: They are the least expensive shoes in the shop.
grammarPointLabel: Superlatif d'infériorité pluriel: les + moins + adj (f. pl.)
explanation: les moins chères = the least expensive (f. pl.). Adjective agrees in gender and number: chaussures (f. pl.) → chères.

quizQuestion:
Ce restaurant est {___} l'autre.
(This restaurant is as good as the other one.)
sentenceTranslation: This restaurant is as good as the other one.
grammarPointLabel: Comparatif d'égalité avec bon: aussi bon que
explanation: aussi bon que = as good as. Note: equality with bon uses aussi bon que, not aussi meilleur que.

quizQuestion:
Ces fleurs sont {___} que celles d'avant.
(These flowers are better than the previous ones.)
sentenceTranslation: These flowers are better than the previous ones.
explanation: meilleures = better (f. pl.). meilleur agrees fully: meilleur (m. sg.) / meilleure (f. sg.) / meilleurs (m. pl.) / meilleures (f. pl.).
